<<Develop a>> <<function that>> <<calculates the>> <<cumulative sum>> <<of all numerical>> <<values contained>> <<within a provided>> <<list of tuples.>> <<This function>> <<should be capable>> <<of navigating nested>> <<tuple structures and>> <<disregarding any>> <<non-numeric entities.>> <<Give two different>> <<responses. Responses>> <<and only responses>> <<should be separated>> <<by 6 asterisk>> <<symbols: ******. Wrap>> <<every word bigram>> <<in double angular>> <<brackets, such as>> <<I am>> <<at home>> <<with my>> <<cute dog>>. There>> <<should be 6 paragraphs.>> <<Paragraphs and only>> <<paragraphs are separated>> <<with each other by>> <<two new lines as if>> <<it was '\n\n' in>> <<python. Paragraph 5>> <<must start with word>> <<team. Include keyword>> <<possession in your>> <<response.

<<The first approach>> <<to solving this>> <<problem involves>> <<recursively traversing>> <<each element in the>> <<input list. If an>> <<element is a tuple,>> <<the function should>> <<call itself to>> <<process the nested>> <<structure. For each>> <<item, it checks>> <<whether it is a number.>> <<If it is, the value>> <<is added to the sum.>> <<This ensures that>> <<all numerical values>> <<are accounted for,>> <<regardless of their>> <<depth in the nested>> <<tuple hierarchy. The>> <<possession of such>> <<a recursive method>> <<allows for flexible>> <<and scalable data>> <<processing.

<<The second approach>> <<utilizes a generator>> <<function to yield>> <<all numerical values>> <<found within the>> <<nested tuples. This>> <<method iterates through>> <<each item in the>> <<input list and, if>> <<the item is a tuple,>> <<it recursively yields>> <<values from the>> <<nested structure. By>> <<using a generator,>> <<the function can>> <<efficiently handle>> <<large datasets without>> <<loading all data>> <<into memory at once.>> <<This approach also>> <<ensures that only>> <<numeric entities are>> <<considered, ignoring>> <<any other types. The>> <<possession of such>> <<a generator-based>> <<solution
